verse_text: To all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

links.context: Peace was the customary Jewish word of both greeting and farewell. Perfect peace is brought by Christ, who reconciles humanity to God ([[EPH.2:14]]). Peace is part of the traditional greeting of Christians to each other ([[ROM.1:7]]; [[1CO.1:3]]), and the greeting "Peace be to all" is offered many times during the liturgical services of the Church.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
